short_description.value: CROMA is a remote sensing foundation model that combines contrastive and reconstruction self-supervised objectives to learn rich unimodal and multimodal representations from spatially aligned multispectral optical and SAR data. It introduces 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi for spatial bias in attention, enabling extrapolation to larger images and outperforming prior state-of-the-art models on multiple benchmarks.

pretext_training_type.value: Joint contrastive and masked autoencoding (reconstruction) self-supervised learning

masking_strategy.value: MAE-style random masking; independently mask 75% of radar and optical patches

pretraining.value: Joint pretraining of three encoders (optical, radar, and joint radar-optical) end-to-end on SSL4EO Sentinel-1 & 2 data, using contrastive and reconstruction objectives with 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi positional encoding.

value.value: Spatial alignment of multimodal remote sensing data
Sensor fusion (Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 multispectral optical)
Relative position encoding for spatial extrapolation
No sinusoidal position embeddings; uses 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi for spatial bias in attention
Global, 250,000 locations sampled around the world, focused on areas surrounding human settlements

time_range.value: Four times per year, spread out over a year (exact years not specified)

value.value: Identical augmentations applied to both modalities

sampling.value: Random spatial and temporal sampling; 250K locations x 4 times

application.value: Land cover and land use classification
Functional Map of the World (fMoW) classification
